name: Rope, 3” hemp, per ft (8,000 lb cap.)

system.docHtml: This immense cable is woven from four to six strands of heavy cordage twisted together, each as thick as a man’s wrist. Used to anchor vessels to moorings, hoist siege equipment, or bind logs for transport downriver, three-inch hemp is the province of ship-masters, engineers, and fortress stewards. New line can hold twice its marked weight before snapping.
